POINTER_RESIDUAL.summary: Dominant class. All seven primary failures still dereference nested aris-active-context/* paths that no longer exist under the external pointer contract.

EXPECTATION_DRIFT.summary: The guard fixture still materializes a nested active-context tree and asserts pass, while the current contract blocks or invalidates that materialization shape.

ROUTE_DRIFT_OR_CANONICAL_TEXT_DRIFT.summary: These suites are pointer-blocked first, but their source modules still encode an IF08/W0.5 live route that conflicts with the preserved PURG-01 live JSON route.

OUT_OF_SCOPE_LEGACY.summary: Both active-track suites also expect pre-F21 artifacts under artifacts/v5/* that are absent in the fresh reclone; that legacy dependency is secondary noise, not the first blocking signal.

UNKNOWN.summary: No focused failing suite remains unclassified.

candidate_future_repair_files_with_evide.why_candidate: The fixture copies the failing nested active-context inputs directly and also preserves the old roadmap fallback path.
The module still hard-codes nested active-context paths and pre-F21 v5 lineage artifacts.
The fixture copies the nested active-context files and the legacy PRE-F21 artifacts directly.
The source still binds resume readiness to nested active-context files and artifacts/v5 PRE-F21 files.
The gate reads DECISION_LOCKS.md and NEXT_ACTION.md only from the removed nested active-context tree.
The fixture explicitly creates a nested active-context directory and still expects pass from that materialization shape.
The guard validates only a nested active-context tree and therefore may now be enforcing a stale contract.
The test copies every artifact required by ARTIFACT_SPECS, including the removed nested active-context files.
ARTIFACT_SPECS still lists the nested active-context files as required closure artifacts.
The packet builder reads nested active-context files first and then still enforces the historical IF08 live route.
The review builder reads nested active-context files and active controlled-execution mirrors before checking a stale IF08 continuation chain.
The controlled execution builder makes nested active-context files part of its required INPUT_PATHS and still preserves IF08 W0.5 route tokens as live expectations.

future_repair_order_recommendation: Open PURG04_MIXED_SURFACE_REPAIR_SPLIT_PACKET before any patch, because the focused surface is not a single-class pointer-only problem.
Split the future work into pointer-residual path-contract normalization first, isolated expectation-drift review second, and IF08 route/canonical-text review third.

Keep tests.test_phase_completion_materialization_guard and scripts/check_phase_completion_materialization.py outside the first pointer-only patch unless an explicit expectation decision proves they belong together.
Revisit the two active-track suites only after deciding whether artifacts/v5 PRE-F21 inputs are still part of the supported lineage or should be pruned as legacy noise.
future_repair_acceptance_criteria: A future bounded repair touches only files listed in candidate_future_repair_files_with_evidence and preserves all currently forbidden files unchanged.
The fresh reclone remains clean before and after the future repair, and no nested aris-active-context/ directory is recreated at repository root.
Pointer-residual suites stop failing on missing aris-active-context/* paths under the external pointer contract.

The phase completion materialization guard is either explicitly re-baselined to current blocking behavior or brought into alignment through a separately justified contract decision; it must not be silently forced green by recreating a nested tree.
IF08 W0.5 suites are re-reviewed against the preserved PURG-01 live route before any pass claim, rather than assuming historical IF08 route text is still live.
No package installation, dependency-file mutation, finding close, remediation apply, runtime execution, or real_apply is used to obtain green focused tests.
future_repair_rejection_criteria: Any future patch recreates a repository-root aris-active-context/ directory inside the fresh reclone to make tests pass.
Any future patch mutates ACTIVE_CONTEXT_STATE.json, BEDROCK_GATE.md, ACTIVE_CONTEXT_POINTER.md, archive/**, excludent/**, secrets/**, .env*, .gitmodules, or dependency files.
A single patch mixes pointer-residual normalization, expectation-drift rebaselining, and IF08 route rewrite without an explicit split packet or equally bounded admission artifact.
A future step treats secondary legacy noise as justification for widening scope into historical suites that were not part of the eight focused failures.
Any step claims baseline green, remediation proven, or finding close from this classification lineage.
Any step requires package installation or dependency mutation to make the focused cleanroom surface admissible.

items: ACTIVE_CONTEXT_STATE.json and README.md still preserve the live route at PURG-01 with latest_completed_next_recommended_step=execute_purg01_controlled_triage_artifact_only.
PURG04 cleanroom lineage now exists only as report-only governance artifacts plus DECISION_LOCKS.md entries and does not modify the live JSON route.
This classification packet records the drift but does not attempt to resolve it because JSON authority remains explicit and unchanged.

artifact_sha256.self_hash_limitation: The terminal file sha256 is reported externally after write because a file cannot embed its own final sha256 without self-reference drift.
